essay_text:
What was the outcome?
When I was seven, death equated life.
My mother crossed her hands in front of her chest and prayed fervently under her breath. Just moments before,
she had carefully presented trays filled with rice, soups, boiled chickens, stir-fries and spring rolls, “so long as the
number of dishes adds up to an odd number,” she specified. It was the way we invited our ancestors to pay a visit
and always stay beside us.
As I grow up, I also shake off myths and fairy tales. Santa isn’t real, nor are spooky man or ghosts. Gradually, my
belief in ancestor worship fades away. It becomes a mindless practice, a rote requirement.
Then, my maternal grandfather dies.
It arrives preparedly and expectedly. Lung cancer has been consuming him, leaving him with fragmented bones
and frail skin. The funeral is plain and quick. At midnight, the car strolls down the highway, empty as my mind
rolling out front. Yesterday, he was still stroking my hair, reciting his poems, telling me and my brother about the
time when he got lost in Paris, brewing milk tea for the two of us. I cannot wrap my head around the pace and
simplicity of my grandfather’s disappearance.
The next morning, my family shows up at my grandparents’ house to clean up. Under the bed, next to his old
cassette player, my grandfather had neatly organized his books alphabetically. I asked mom if I could keep them;
she nodded without hesitation.
By the window, I carefully flip through pages of Tho Tình, a compilation of love poems, and trace his annotations.
His insight as a literature teacher enriches each poem, magnifying its intricacy and sentiments. All of the sudden,
words sound like music, rushing to my fingertips: I have to write something. My typing does not cease after a
thousand fictional words about my grandfather’s teaching career and his internal conflict with love through
literature. The story flows so naturally, as if my grandfather is right next to me, living on the pristine pages, filling in
the blanks that are missing from myself.
I begin devouring books, jotting down thoughts my grandfather would have had. I begin emitting words, releasing
the emotions I carry with every keystroke and bridging connections I had lost. The comfort of being able to face my
feelings and fears inspired me to take my stories into unexpected directions. The different points of view from the
authors provide me with contrasting opinions and allowing me to make peace with them. Gradually, writing
becomes my coping mechanism, my sense of the world, my best friend. My habit has been kept personal, hidden
within myself, until my school publication, recruits me to their reporting staff. I begin sharing my
opinions and my stories with the small community of Holderness. I receive commentaries on how my stories have
changed someone’s beliefs. That moment exposes me to the power of my stories: they can alter ideas, opinions,
and even actions.
As my love for writing develops, my connection to my grandfather and my acceptance of the tradition of
worshipping my ancestors also reemerge. It is not a myth or a meaningless practice, but rather a way of keeping
memories of our loved ones and connecting with their spirits. Having them physically absent from me should not
mean that they completely disappear; rather, their legacies live on inside us. My grandfather breathes his love of
literature into me even after he passed away. Emerging from the pain of his passing, I also learn that it is only
natural that people will depart from my life, and it only means I should cherish them as long as they are still
present in my life and even when they are gone. The loss fosters the growth of both me and my writing.
9
As they lay flowers on his shrine, I will lay words on his memory.
prompt: Reflect on a time when you questioned or challenged a belief or idea. What prompted your thinking?
persona_prompt: You are a writer who crafts reflective and earnest narratives, often beginning with a striking question or statement. Weave personal anecdotes and emotional vulnerabilities with philosophical insights, employing a circular structure that demonstrates growth and evolved understanding. Use a blend of concise, impactful sentences and longer, more descriptive ones, opting for precise vocabulary that conveys depth without resorting to jargon.
sentence_style: Varies significantly; intersperses short, impactful sentences (sometimes fragments for dramatic effect) with longer, more flowing sentences that build description or philosophical reflection. Possesses a natural rhythm, particularly in lists or parallel structures.
vocabulary_level: Moderate to Sophisticated — utilizes precise verbs and nouns to convey nuanced meaning and emotion, but remains highly accessible and avoids overly academic or obscure language.
structural_patterns: Employs a circular narrative, beginning and ending with a similar theme (ancestor worship) but demonstrating significant growth in understanding. Moves chronologically through personal experience, using anecdotal evidence to illustrate evolving beliefs and internal transformations. Frequently uses contrast to highlight shifts in perspective (e.g., childhood belief vs. adult skepticism).
distinctive_moves: Opens with an intriguing rhetorical question or a concise, impactful philosophical statement.
Uses lists and parallel structures to build rhythm and emphasize a series of memories, actions, or realizations.
Grounds abstract philosophical observations in concrete personal experiences and sensory details.
Employs figurative language (similes, metaphors) to articulate internal states or abstract concepts vividly.
Closes with a powerful, often poetic, full-circle statement that reinterprets the initial theme with newfound wisdom.
themes_and_preoccupations: The evolution of belief systems and the reinterpretation of tradition.
Grief, loss, and the enduring legacy of loved ones.
The transformative and redemptive power of writing and literature.
Personal growth and self-discovery through adversity and introspection.
self_presentation: Vulnerable and introspective, openly sharing moments of skepticism, grief, and self-doubt. Presents as a reflective individual on a journey of personal and intellectual evolution, ultimately emerging with a clear sense of purpose and a quiet confidence.
cultural_or_contextual_markers: References to ancestor worship, specific cultural practices (e.g., odd number of dishes), and non-English literary titles ('Tho Tình') anchor the narrative in a distinct cultural background, likely East Asian or Southeast Asian.
avoid_in_imitation: Overly casual or colloquial language that would diminish the reflective and earnest tone.
A purely linear, dispassionate recounting of events without emotional or philosophical depth.
Excessive jargon or academic abstraction that disconnects from the personal narrative.
sample_sentence: What once seemed a rigid, ancient ritual now unfurls as a tender language, bridging the silent expanse between past reverence and present understanding.
